steps.title: Read the gene pattern
steps.content: Duval and colleagues found anterior genes active across the sea star's entire body, posterior genes limited to the edges, and no trunk-related pattern. That finding challenges the old idea that sea stars became headless.

steps.title: Choose the supported inference
steps.content: Choice A invents a claim about posterior genes causing the starlike layout. Choice B rejects the known bilateral origin. Choice C says the genetic markers barely correspond, even though the comparison depends on their correspondence. The remaining choice matches the head-region and no-trunk finding. The answer is D.
